title: Affirm (AFRM) Q2 Earnings Preview
text: Affirm, the buy-now-pay-later platform, will report Q2 earnings Thursday after market close. Analysts anticipate revenue of $837.4 million, representing 27% year on year growth, with adjusted EPS expected at $0.43 per share. Last quarter, the company delivered mixed results. While it met analysts' revenue expectations, posting $783.1 million in revenue with 35.9% year-over-year growth, it significantly missed EPS estimates. Peer performance offers insight. LendingClub beat revenue expectations by 9.2% with 32.7% growth, while Dave exceeded estimates by 16% with 64.5% growth. Market reactions varied—LendingClub stock rose 20.9% post-earnings while Dave fell 19.9%. Affirm shares have gained 15.7% over the past month, outperforming the personal loan segment's average 2.4% gain. The stock trades near the average analyst price target of $76.68. Analysts have reconfirmed their estimates over the last 30 days, suggesting confidence in expectations, though the company has missed Wall Street's revenue estimates twice over the last two years.
